templates.body:
FINANCE REQUEST · [Business]
Job/PO: _______________  Amount: _______________  Date: _______________
Vendor/customer: _______________
Purpose (one line): _______________
Attachment: receipt / invoice / quote (Y/N)

Ask: what GL/approval/code is required before we proceed?
Following up finance ticket [id]. Attached: [receipt/PO]. Ready for your code or approval step. No rush on vibes; need your checklist item: _______________

HR REQUEST (written only)
Employee/id: _______________  Topic: _______________  Dates: _______________

Question: _______________

Not a vent session. Requesting form, policy cite, or next step per handbook.
I want to keep this professional and documented. Happy to stick to facts on [topic] in writing. If you need a formal report I can file one.

DOCK CHECK · Job/SO: _______________  SKU/desc: _______________

What do you see on the floor for this? Inbound ETA? Outbound ready date?

I will not tell the customer a date until you confirm.
Thanks for the floor truth on [job]. Customer updated with your confirmed date only. Flag me if inbound slips.
Quick clarify on [low hanging fruit / teamwork / priority]: which single task, owner, and due date should I execute first? I want it logged so we are aligned.

TWO-LANE ISSUE
1) Shipping: floor status for [job] — need truth before customer reply.
2) Finance: [invoice/payment] — need code/approval with receipt attached.

Order: dock confirm first, then AR/AP step.
